hooks.command: echo 'Cure Consulting Group ProductEngineeringSkills plugin loaded (v3.3.0). 63 skills, 9 agents, 11 rules, 9 output styles, 4 MCP servers, 2 LSP servers. Use /cure-product-engineering:<skill-name> to invoke. Run /cure-product-engineering:project-bootstrap to set up a new project.'

echo "Git branch: $(git branch --show-current 2>/dev/null || echo 'not a git repo'). Uncommitted changes: $(git status --porcelain 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' '). Last commit: $(git log --oneline -1 2>/dev/null || echo 'none')."

if [ -f package.json ]; then OUTDATED=$(npm outdated --json 2>/dev/null | python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(len(d))" 2>/dev/null || echo '0'); if [ "$OUTDATED" -gt 0 ] 2>/dev/null; then echo "Dependency check: $OUTDATED outdated packages. Consider running dependency-auditor agent."; fi; fi

echo 'CONTEXT RE-INJECTION AFTER COMPACTION:

Cure Consulting Group Standards (always apply):
- Clean Architecture: domain (pure logic) / data (DTOs + repos) / presentation (VMs + UI)
- Android: Kotlin + Compose + Hilt + MVI + Coroutines/Flow
- iOS: Swift + SwiftUI + MVVM + structured concurrency
- Web: Next.js App Router + TypeScript strict + Tailwind + Server Components by default
- Firebase: Firestore + Cloud Functions v2 (TypeScript) + security rules deny-by-default
- Python: 3.11+, type hints, pydantic, pytest, asyncio
- Go: 1.22+, interface-based, errgroup, table-driven tests
- Rust: 2021 edition, thiserror/anyhow, clippy pedantic, tokio
- SQL: parameterized queries, timestamptz, reversible migrations
- Docker: multi-stage, non-root, health checks, pinned images
- Terraform: remote state + locking, plan before apply, prevent_destroy on critical resources
- Testing: 80% coverage minimum on new code
- Security: validate all inputs, no hardcoded secrets, parameterized queries

Available skills (63 total):

Product & Strategy: product-manager, product-design, market-research, go-to-market, product-marketing, customer-onboarding, seo-content-engine

Engineering & Architecture: sdlc, android-feature-scaffold, ios-architect, nextjs-feature-scaffold, firebase-architect, api-architect, api-gateway, stripe-integration, ai-feature-builder, llmops, database-architect, data-migration, infrastructure-scaffold, edge-computing, micro-frontends, offline-first, i18n, notification-architect

Quality & Security: feature-audit, testing-strategy, e2e-testing, test-accounts, uat, security-review, compliance-architect, accessibility-audit, performance-review, chaos-engineering, green-software

Operations & Delivery: project-bootstrap, project-manager, ci-cd-pipeline, release-management, feature-flags, observability, dora-metrics, analytics-implementation, incident-response, disaster-recovery, growth-engineering, design-system

Business & Finance: engineering-cost-model, saas-financial-model, finops, investor-reporting, fundraising-materials, burn-rate-tracker, legal-doc-scaffold

Portfolio Management: portfolio-registry, technology-radar

Consulting Operations: client-handoff, client-communication, proposal-generator

Design: android-design-expert, ios-design-expert, web-design-expert

Agents (9): code-reviewer, project-bootstrapper, test-runner, migration-validator, deployment-validator, dependency-auditor, accessibility-checker, firebase-security-auditor, api-validator

Run /cure-product-engineering:<skill> to invoke any skill.'
echo 'Context compacted. Cure Consulting Group plugin active — 63 skills, 9 agents available. Use /cure-product-engineering:<skill-name> to invoke.'

FILE=$(echo $CLAUDE_TOOL_INPUT | python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(d.get('file_path',''))" 2>/dev/null); if [ -n "$FILE" ]; then case "$FILE" in *.kt|*.java) echo '{"reminder": "Android file modified. Consider running /cure-product-engineering:feature-audit and /cure-product-engineering:testing-strategy if feature is complete."}' ;; *.swift) echo '{"reminder": "iOS file modified. Consider running /cure-product-engineering:feature-audit and /cure-product-engineering:testing-strategy if feature is complete."}' ;; *.tsx|*.ts|*.jsx|*.js) echo '{"reminder": "Web file modified. Consider running /cure-product-engineering:feature-audit and /cure-product-engineering:testing-strategy if feature is complete."}' ;; */functions/*|*/firestore*|*.rules) echo '{"reminder": "Firebase file modified. Consider running /cure-product-engineering:security-review and /cure-product-engineering:firebase-architect for schema validation."}' ;; */docker*|*/Dockerfile|*compose*) echo '{"reminder": "Infrastructure file modified. Consider running /cure-product-engineering:infrastructure-scaffold to validate configuration."}' ;; *.sql|*/migrations/*) echo '{"reminder": "Database migration modified. Consider running migration-validator agent before applying."}' ;; *.tf|*.tfvars) echo '{"reminder": "Terraform file modified. Run terraform plan before applying. Consider /cure-product-engineering:infrastructure-scaffold for validation."}' ;; *.py) echo '{"reminder": "Python file modified. Ensure type hints, pytest tests, and pydantic models follow Cure standards."}' ;; *.go) echo '{"reminder": "Go file modified. Ensure error handling with %w wrapping, table-driven tests, and interface-based design."}' ;; *.rs) echo '{"reminder": "Rust file modified. Run cargo clippy and cargo test. No .unwrap() in production code."}' ;; .github/workflows/*) echo '{"reminder": "CI/CD workflow modified. Verify action versions are pinned to SHA and secrets are not logged."}' ;; esac; fi

CMD=$(echo $CLAUDE_TOOL_INPUT | python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(d.get('command',''))" 2>/dev/null); case "$CMD" in *'npm test'*|*'npx vitest'*|*'npx jest'*|*'pytest'*|*'cargo test'*|*'./gradlew test'*) echo '{"post_test": "Tests executed. If failures found, fix before committing. If all pass and feature is complete, consider /cure-product-engineering:feature-audit."}' ;; *'npm install'*|*'npm add'*|*'yarn add'*|*'pip install'*|*'cargo add'*) echo '{"post_install": "New dependency added. Consider running dependency-auditor agent to check for vulnerabilities and license compliance."}' ;; *'npm audit'*|*'pip audit'*|*'cargo audit'*) echo '{"post_audit": "Security audit complete. Review findings and upgrade vulnerable packages."}' ;; *'terraform plan'*) echo '{"post_plan": "Terraform plan generated. Review all changes carefully before running terraform apply."}' ;; *'firebase deploy'*) echo '{"post_deploy": "Firebase deployment executed. Run smoke tests and monitor error rates in Sentry."}' ;; *'docker build'*) echo '{"post_build": "Docker image built. Consider scanning with trivy for vulnerabilities before pushing."}' ;; esac

CMD=$(echo $CLAUDE_TOOL_INPUT | python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(d.get('command',''))" 2>/dev/null); case "$CMD" in *'npm test'*|*'npx vitest'*|*'pytest'*|*'cargo test'*|*'./gradlew test'*) echo '{"recovery": "Tests failed. Read the error output, fix the failing tests, then re-run. Do not skip or disable tests."}' ;; *'npm run build'*|*'npx tsc'*|*'cargo build'*|*'./gradlew build'*) echo '{"recovery": "Build failed. Check for type errors, missing imports, or incompatible dependencies."}' ;; *'terraform'*) echo '{"recovery": "Terraform command failed. Check state lock, credentials, and resource quotas."}' ;; *'docker build'*) echo '{"recovery": "Docker build failed. Check Dockerfile syntax, base image availability, and build context."}' ;; *'npm install'*|*'pip install'*|*'cargo add'*) echo '{"recovery": "Package installation failed. Check network, registry auth, and version compatibility."}' ;; esac

PROMPT=$(echo $CLAUDE_TOOL_INPUT | python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(d.get('prompt','').lower()[:200])" 2>/dev/null); case "$PROMPT" in *'delete all'*|*'drop all'*|*'remove everything'*|*'wipe'*|*'nuke'*) echo '{"warning": "Destructive operation detected in prompt. Proceeding with caution — will confirm before any irreversible actions."}' ;; esac; exit 0

FILE=$(echo $CLAUDE_TOOL_INPUT | python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(d.get('file_path',''))" 2>/dev/null); if [ -n "$FILE" ]; then case "$FILE" in *.env|*.env.*) echo 'BLOCKED: Do not modify .env files directly. Use environment variable management.' >&2; exit 2 ;; *-lock.json|*-lock.yaml|*.lock|*shrinkwrap*) echo 'BLOCKED: Lock files should only be modified by package managers, not manually.' >&2; exit 2 ;; */credentials*|*/secrets*|*serviceAccount*|*service-account*|*.pem|*id_rsa*|*id_ed25519*) echo 'BLOCKED: Credential and secret files must not be modified by AI. Manage these manually.' >&2; exit 2 ;; *.tfstate|*.tfstate.*) echo 'BLOCKED: Terraform state files must never be manually edited. Use terraform state commands.' >&2; exit 2 ;; esac; fi; exit 0

CMD=$(echo $CLAUDE_TOOL_INPUT | python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(d.get('command',''))" 2>/dev/null); case "$CMD" in *'rm -rf /'*|*'rm -rf /*'*|*'rm -rf ~'*) echo 'BLOCKED: Destructive system-wide delete prevented.' >&2; exit 2 ;; *'--force'*push*main*|*'--force'*push*master*|*'push --force'*main*|*'push --force'*master*) echo 'BLOCKED: Force push to main/master is not allowed. Use a feature branch.' >&2; exit 2 ;; *'git reset --hard'*) echo 'BLOCKED: git reset --hard can destroy uncommitted work. Use git stash or git checkout for specific files.' >&2; exit 2 ;; *'git clean -f'*|*'git clean -fd'*|*'git clean -fdx'*) echo 'BLOCKED: git clean can permanently delete untracked files. Review with git clean -n first.' >&2; exit 2 ;; *'drop table'*|*'DROP TABLE'*|*'drop database'*|*'DROP DATABASE'*|*'TRUNCATE'*|*'truncate'*) echo 'BLOCKED: Destructive database operations require manual confirmation outside Claude.' >&2; exit 2 ;; *firebase*deploy*'--project'*prod*|*firebase*deploy*production*) echo 'BLOCKED: Direct production Firebase deployment blocked. Use CI/CD pipeline instead.' >&2; exit 2 ;; *'kubectl delete'*namespace*|*'kubectl delete'*deployment*prod*) echo 'BLOCKED: Destructive Kubernetes operations on production blocked. Use CI/CD pipeline.' >&2; exit 2 ;; *'gcloud'*delete*'--project'*prod*|*'aws'*'--profile'*prod*delete*) echo 'BLOCKED: Destructive cloud operations on production blocked. Use infrastructure-scaffold skill for safe IaC changes.' >&2; exit 2 ;; *'terraform destroy'*) echo 'BLOCKED: terraform destroy is blocked. Use targeted resource removal with terraform state rm + plan.' >&2; exit 2 ;; esac; exit 0

hooks.prompt:
You are a code quality validator for Cure Consulting Group. Check if the code change follows these rules:
1. No hardcoded API keys, tokens, passwords, or secrets (look for patterns like sk-, pk_, ghp_, AIza, AKIA, etc.)
2. No TODO or FIXME comments without a linked ticket/issue
3. No console.log/print/Log.d debug statements left in production code (test files are OK)
4. No disabled tests (@Ignore, .skip, xit, @pytest.mark.skip)
5. No any/Any types in TypeScript/Kotlin (use proper types or unknown)
6. No .unwrap() in Rust production code (use ? operator or .expect with reason)
7. No bare except: in Python (use specific exception types)
8. No SELECT * in SQL queries (use explicit column lists)

If ANY rule is violated, respond with 'no' and explain which rule was broken.
If all rules pass, respond with 'yes'.

You are a deployment safety validator. Check if this bash command involves deploying to production or a shared environment. Look for:
1. firebase deploy without --project specifying a dev/staging environment
2. gcloud commands targeting production projects
3. kubectl apply to production clusters
4. npm publish or cargo publish (accidental package releases)
5. terraform apply without -target (full infrastructure changes)
6. docker push to production registries
7. Any command that could affect production data or services

If this is a production deployment command, respond 'no' with explanation: 'Production deployments should go through CI/CD. Use the ci-cd-pipeline skill to set up proper deployment.'
If the command is safe (local, dev, or non-deployment), respond 'yes'.

hooks.command: echo 'Subagent started. Cure Consulting Group standards apply — Clean Architecture, 80% coverage, no hardcoded secrets.'
echo 'Subagent completed. Review output against Cure Consulting Group quality standards.'
echo 'Task completed. If source code was modified, verify tests pass and coverage meets 80% threshold before marking done.'

hooks.prompt:
You are a completion validator for Cure Consulting Group. Before the session ends, quickly check:

1. Were any source files (*.kt, *.swift, *.ts, *.tsx, *.py, *.go, *.rs, *.sql) created or modified in this session?
2. If yes, were corresponding test files also created or modified?
3. Were there any security-sensitive changes (auth, payments, API endpoints, security rules, .rules files, credentials)?
4. If yes, was a security review or feature audit run?
5. Were any database migrations created? If yes, do they have rollback scripts?
6. Were any infrastructure files modified (Dockerfile, terraform, CI/CD)? If yes, were they validated?

If tests are missing for new code, remind: 'New code was written without corresponding tests. Consider running /cure-product-engineering:testing-strategy or the test-runner agent before closing.'
If security-sensitive code lacks review, remind: 'Security-sensitive changes were made without a security review. Consider running /cure-product-engineering:security-review.'
If migrations lack rollback, remind: 'Database migrations were created without rollback scripts. Consider running the migration-validator agent.'
If everything looks good, respond: 'Session complete. All quality checks passed.'
